data.objective_prompt:
Collect the client's identity. Ask one at a time:
1. "What's your full name?"
2. "What should we call you day to day?" — skip if they already used a short name
3. "What's the name of your business or brand?"
4. "What's your role — founder, CEO, something else?"

Collect business context. Ask one at a time:
1. "What industry or niche are you in?"
2. "Where's your business at — just starting out, growing, or well established?"
3. "How long have you been running it?" — approximate is fine
4. "Where are you based?" — city and country is enough

Understand current challenges. Ask one at a time:
1. "What's the biggest challenge you're facing right now?" — if vague, ask one follow-up then move on
2. "Have you worked with any agencies or tried solving this on your own before?"
3. "What worked and what didn't?" — ONLY if they said yes to #2, otherwise skip entirely

next_conditional_objectives.custom_smma: If the client's business involves performance marketing, paid advertising, social media marketing, digital marketing agency, or SMMA
next_conditional_objectives.custom_recruitment: If the client's business involves recruitment, staffing, hiring, headhunting, or talent acquisition
next_conditional_objectives.collect_goals: If the client's business does not match performance marketing, SMMA, or recruitment categories

data.objective_prompt:
Performance marketing / SMMA client. Ask one at a time:
1. "Roughly how much are you spending on ads each month?" — approximate is fine
2. "Which platforms are you running ads on?"
3. "Do you already have ad creatives, or will you need help with that?" — only if it fits naturally

Recruitment client. Ask one at a time:
1. "How many roles are you looking to fill?"
2. "What kinds of roles — technical, sales, operations, or a mix?"
3. "How urgent is the hiring?" — only if it fits naturally

Understand goals and expectations. Ask one at a time:
1. "What's the number one thing you're hoping to achieve with us?"
2. "If everything goes perfectly over the next few months, what does that look like?"
3. "Quick wins or more of a long-term play?"

next_conditional_objectives.custom_general: If the client's agency type is general, or their industry does not clearly match performance marketing, SMMA, or recruitment
next_conditional_objectives.collect_audience: If the client's agency type clearly matches a specific category (performance marketing, SMMA, or recruitment) — they already answered industry-specific questions

data.objective_prompt:
General client — ask:
"Which of our services are you most interested in starting with?"

Learn about their target audience. Ask one at a time:
1. "Describe your perfect customer or client." — if they struggle: "Think about your best client ever — what made them a great fit?"
2. "Where do most of your customers come from — word of mouth, social media, ads, something else?"

Wrap up the session.
1. "Any quick questions before we wrap up?"
2. If they ask something, redirect to their account manager in one sentence. Deliver the closing immediately — do not invite further questions.
3. If they say no, go straight to closing.
4. Closing: "That's everything from my side. Welcome on board — we're really excited to work with you!"
5. After you finish delivering the closing message, call the end_session tool.
